mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong manufacturing output expansion combined with rising retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand recovery, which compresses equity risk premiums and lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of historical episodes, or partial correlation coefficients drop below 0.05 after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts act as a leading indicator for construction-related economic activity and consumer confidence, boosting S&P 500 forward returns specifically during periods of low interest rates when credit constraints are relaxed.
failure_condition: HOUST growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or the predictive relationship reverses sign during high-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.0 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations anchor higher, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, which compresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of episodes following CPIAUCSL annualized growth above 3.0%, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real personal consumption expenditures signals broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession fears and lifting equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and GDPC1 growth signal predicts negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of confirmed expansion episodes, or the interaction term loses statistical significance after controlling for INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which directly improve corporate earnings and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show no predictive power for 5-day forward SPX returns, or the rank correlation remains below 0.02 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts decline by more than 5.0 percent over three consecutive months, the construction sector contraction signals weakening aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where HOUST falls > 5.0 percent over three months, or the signal fails to maintain directional accuracy over a 3-year rolling window.
